rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Order Expunging Improperly Recorded Lis Pendens Against Receivership Real Property Located At 1135 1139 Guerrero Street San Francisco California
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 1, 2021 line 1. RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Order Expunging Improperly Recorded Lis Pendens Against Receivership Real Property Located At 1135-1139 Guerrero Street San Francisco California is GRANTED. Late filed opposition is stricken. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CUONG VAN TRAN VS. RAMON PARAS-NISCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff Cuong Van Tran'S Motion To Amend Judgment.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 1, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF CUONG TRAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment is DENIED. This motion was filed on May 14, 2021 seeking to amend a judgment entered on June 1, 2021. There is no judgment dated June 1, 2021 on the record. To the extent this motion is seeking to amend a judgment that may be entered on June 1, 2021, moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that such amendment is appropriate on necessary under Code of Civil Procedure and Probate Code. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JOANNA PFEISTER VS. ACTION PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 1, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT ACTION PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'s DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege any viable cause of action against the defendant. Amendments shall be made in good faith in accordance with the Court's January 13, 2021 ruling and authorities regarding standing cited by the Defendants. Each cause of action (existing or new) shall be separately stated and facts in support of each cause of action shall be alleged in clear and concise language, including but not limited to facts supporting standing as to each breach/violation/wrongdoing; each breach/violation/wrongdoing; damages caused by each breached/violation/wrongdoing; and causation. Additionally, any references to Corporations Code 5115(c) and Penal Code Sections 422.6, 368 and 241.8 shall be removed from the Second Amended Complaint. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
